questions.q: Angles on a straight line are $71^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Subtracts from $90$ degrees instead of the straight-line total.
Subtracts from $360$ degrees instead of the straight-line total.
Assumes adjacent angles are equal rather than supplementary.

questions.q: A right angle is split into $34^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Subtracts from $180$ degrees instead of the right-angle total.
Subtracts from $360$ degrees instead of the right-angle total.
Assumes the two parts of a right angle are equal.

questions.q: Angles at a point are $95^{\circ}$, $120^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Subtracts the known angles from $250$ instead of $360$ degrees.
Uses a straight-line total of $180$ degrees for angles at a point.
Adds the known angles but does not subtract from the full turn.

questions.q: One angle is vertically opposite an angle of $133^{\circ}$. What is its measure?

options.why: Treats vertically opposite angles as a supplementary adjacent pair.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of using equal opposite angles.
Assumes an intersection automatically creates right angles.

questions.q: One angle is split into $29^{\circ}$ and $52^{\circ}$. Find its vertically opposite angle.

options.why: Finds the supplementary adjacent angle after adding the split parts.
Subtracts the split parts instead of combining the whole angle.
Subtracts the whole split angle from a full turn.
